title: Lithocarpus caudatifolius
intro: Lithocarpus caudatifolius, also known as the Taiwan Lithocarpus, is a species of evergreen tree native to Taiwan, belonging to the beech family Fagaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Taiwanese medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various health conditions such as fever, sore throats, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus caudatifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lithocarpus caudatifolius is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction to treat various ailments, and its extracts are also used in modern pharmacology for their potential therapeutic applications.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus caudatifolius is classified in the family Fagaceae. This family is commonly known as the beech or oak family, and it consists of about 30 genera. Within the Fagaceae family, Lithocarpus caudatifolius is a species of evergreen tree. The genus Lithocarpus is native to Asia, and it is a group of trees that are often characterized by their distinctive leaves and cones. The classification of Lithocarpus caudatifolius is further supported by its phylogenetic relationships with other species in the Fagaceae family.
